datasets.name: site_capacity_compare
queryLines:
WITH hourly AS (
  SELECT site_id, date, HOUR(started_at) AS h,
         COUNT(DISTINCT agent_id) AS active_agents
  FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
  WHERE date >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
  GROUP BY site_id, date, HOUR(started_at)
),
peak AS (
  SELECT site_id, MAX(active_agents) AS peak_agents
  FROM hourly GROUP BY site_id
)
SELECT s.site_id, 'Peak agents (concurrent)' AS metric, p.peak_agents AS value
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_sites s JOIN peak p USING (site_id)
UNION ALL
SELECT s.site_id, 'Capacity (provisioned)' AS metric, s.capacity AS value
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_sites s

datasets.name: sla_heatmap_data
queryLines:
SELECT date, site_id,
       CASE
         WHEN AVG(meets_sla) < 0.55 THEN 0.10
         WHEN AVG(meets_sla) < 0.70 THEN 0.40
         WHEN AVG(meets_sla) < 0.80 THEN 0.65
         ELSE                              0.95
       END AS sla_band,
       AVG(meets_sla) AS sla_actual
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
WHERE date >= current_date() - INTERVAL 180 DAYS
GROUP BY date, site_id

datasets.name: queue_wait_compare
queryLines:
WITH max_date AS (
  SELECT MAX(date) AS d FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
  WHERE date >= current_date() - INTERVAL 180 DAYS
)
SELECT queue, 'Last 7 days' AS period, AVG(wait_time_sec) AS wait_sec
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide, max_date
WHERE channel IN ('voice','chat')
  AND date >  max_date.d - 7
  AND date <= max_date.d
GROUP BY queue
UNION ALL
SELECT queue, 'Prior 7 days' AS period, AVG(wait_time_sec) AS wait_sec
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide, max_date
WHERE channel IN ('voice','chat')
  AND date >  max_date.d - 14
  AND date <= max_date.d - 7
GROUP BY queue

datasets.name: wide_calls
queryLines:
SELECT call_id, started_at, ended_at, date, week, channel, queue, topic, agent_id, agent_name, team, hire_date, full_time, tenure_days, tenure_bucket, site_id, region, timezone, site_capacity, handle_time_sec, handle_time_min, wait_time_sec, wait_time_min, wait_time_voice_chat_sec, abandoned, csat_score, sentiment_score, ai_summary, ai_sentiment_label, meets_sla, is_abandoned, is_negative
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
WHERE date >= current_date() - INTERVAL 180 DAYS

columns.description: Voice/chat wait — last 7 days of data.
columns.expression: CASE WHEN date > MAX(date) OVER () - 7 AND channel IN ('voice','chat') THEN CAST(wait_time_sec AS DOUBLE) END
CASE WHEN date > MAX(date) OVER () - 14 AND date <= MAX(date) OVER () - 7 AND channel IN ('voice','chat') THEN CAST(wait_time_sec AS DOUBLE) END

datasets.name: forecast_volume
queryLines:
SELECT ds, y_hat, y_hat_lower, y_hat_upper
FROM main.emma_stowell.gold_forecast_volume
WHERE :target_sla > 0 AND :assumed_aht > 0 AND :shrinkage >= 0
ORDER BY ds

columns.description: Agents (FTE) needed per day at given shift length.

datasets.name: emerging_topics
queryLines:
WITH weekly AS (
  SELECT date_trunc('week', date) AS week, topic,
         AVG(sentiment_score) AS w_sentiment
  FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
  GROUP BY 1, 2
)
SELECT topic,
       w_sentiment AS latest_sentiment,
       LAG(w_sentiment) OVER (PARTITION BY topic ORDER BY week) AS prior_sentiment,
       w_sentiment - LAG(w_sentiment) OVER (PARTITION BY topic ORDER BY week) AS wow_delta
FROM weekly
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY topic ORDER BY week DESC) = 1

datasets.name: ai_summaries
queryLines:
SELECT call_id, topic,
       ROUND(sentiment_score, 2) AS sentiment_score,
       ai_sentiment_label,
       LEFT(ai_summary, 200) AS summary
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
WHERE ai_summary IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sentiment_score ASC
LIMIT 20

datasets.name: metrics_list
queryLines: SELECT * FROM VALUES("SLA"),("AHT"),("Wait Time"),("CSAT Score") AS data(metric_list)

datasets.name: metric_drilldown
queryLines:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN :date_dimension = "Month" THEN DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', date)
    WHEN :date_dimension = "Week"  THEN DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', date)
    WHEN :date_dimension = "Day"   THEN date
  END AS grouped_date,
  count(call_id)         AS calls_handled,
  avg(meets_sla)         AS service_level,
  avg(wait_time_sec)     AS wait_time,
  avg(handle_time_sec)   AS AHT,
  avg(csat_score)        AS CSAT_score
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
WHERE date >= '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY grouped_date

columns.description: derrives the metric for the drop down
columns.expression: CASE WHEN :choose_metric = 'SLA' THEN service_level WHEN :choose_metric = 'AHT' THEN AHT WHEN :choose_metric = 'Wait Time' THEN wait_time WHEN :choose_metric = 'CSAT Score' THEN csat_score END
CASE WHEN :choose_metric = 'SLA' THEN 0.8 WHEN :choose_metric = 'AHT' THEN 300 WHEN :choose_metric = 'Wait Time' THEN 20 WHEN :choose_metric = 'CSAT Score' THEN 5 END

datasets.name: agent_league
queryLines:
SELECT agent_name, site_id, tenure_bucket,
       COUNT(*)                            AS calls,
       AVG(handle_time_sec)                AS avg_handle_sec,
       AVG(wait_time_voice_chat_sec)       AS avg_wait_vc_sec,
       AVG(meets_sla)                      AS sla_pct,
       AVG(csat_score)                     AS avg_csat,
       AVG(sentiment_score)                AS avg_sentiment,
       AVG(is_negative)                    AS neg_share
FROM main.emma_stowell.silver_calls_wide
WHERE date >= current_date() - INTERVAL 180 DAYS
GROUP BY agent_name, site_id, tenure_bucket

lines:
### Overview
_How's the contact centre this week?_

frame.title: Total calls (90d)
frame.description: Total inbound calls handled across all channels in the last 90 days. Delta chip shows percent change vs the previous day.

frame.title: SLA % (avg 90d)
frame.description: Share of calls answered within channel-aware SLA windows. Coloured green ≥80%, amber 70-80%, red <70%.

frame.title: Avg Handle Time (s)
frame.description: Average call handle time in seconds (lower is better). Green ≤300s, amber 300-420s, red >420s.

frame.title: Avg Wait — voice/chat (s)
frame.description: Average speed of answer for voice & chat in seconds. Green ≤30s, amber 30-60s, red ≥60s.

frame.title: CSAT (current month)
frame.description: Average customer satisfaction score (1-5 scale) for the current month. Delta chip shows percent change vs 3 months ago — spans the Q1/Q2 boundary so reads as quarter-over-quarter. Industry target ≥4.0 — green ≥4.0, amber 3.5-4.0, red <3.5.

frame.title: Abandon % (avg 90d)
frame.description: Share of calls where the customer hung up before being answered. Green ≤5%, amber 5-10%, red ≥10%.

frame.title: Volume by channel · 90 days
frame.description: Daily call volume stacked by channel (voice / chat / email). Use to spot channel-mix shifts and demand spikes over the last 90 days. Hover for SLA per channel/day — the email anomaly (best CSAT, worst SLA) shows up here.

frame.title: Top topics · 90 days
frame.description: Top call topics by volume over the last 90 days. Longest bars are the dominant drivers of contact volume. Hover for CSAT — the manager's punch is when the biggest topic is also the unhappiest.

frame.title: Sentiment trend by topic · 6 months (monthly)
frame.description: Monthly average sentiment per topic on a -1 (very negative) to +1 (very positive) scale. Lines drifting downward flag topics where customer sentiment is degrading. Hover to see the call volume behind each point — small samples can swing wildly.

lines:
### Operations
_Where are we breaking SLA and why?_

frame.title: SLA heatmap · sites × day
frame.description: SLA % by site (rows) and date (cols). Red rows = chronic underperformers; vertical red columns = stress events affecting all sites at once.

frame.title: Queue wait — last 7 days vs prior 7 (voice/chat, s)
frame.description: Average voice/chat wait time per queue, comparing the last 7 days vs the 7 days before. Dark navy bars taller than light blue = queues getting slower week on week.

frame.title: Handle-time distribution by channel
frame.description: Box plot of handle time per channel — box = Q1-Q3, line = median, whiskers = min/max. Tall boxes mean inconsistent handling; voice should be tightest, email widest.

frame.title: Selected Metric Over Time
frame.description: Selected metric (bars) plotted against its target line over the chosen date granularity. Pick a metric and granularity below — bars below the target line indicate underperformance.

lines:
### Agents
_Who needs coaching, where do we need coverage?_

frame.title: Agent cohorts: handle time × CSAT (site × tenure)
frame.description: Each bubble is one (site × tenure) cohort. X = avg handle time, Y = avg CSAT, size = call volume. Bottom-right = high handle time + low satisfaction = coaching opportunity; top-left = top performers.

frame.title: Site peak agents vs capacity
frame.description: Per site: peak concurrent agents (busiest hour, last 30 days) vs the site's provisioned capacity. The headroom between the two bars tells you whether the site is running tight (small headroom) or overprovisioned (large headroom — Toledo's expansion-site story).

frame.title: Agent league table
frame.description: Per-agent KPIs over the last 180 days. Cells coloured red/amber/green against the same thresholds as the Overview KPI tiles — sort or filter to find coaching candidates.

lines:
### Voice of Customer
_What are callers actually saying?_

frame.title: Topic → Channel flow
frame.description: Flow of calls from topic (left) into channel (right), bar thickness = call volume. Use to see which topics map to which channel — e.g. billing → voice, password reset → chat.

frame.title: Topic sentiment trend · 6 months (monthly)
frame.description: Monthly average sentiment per topic on a -1 to +1 scale. Lines trending down = topics where customer sentiment is degrading and need attention. Hover to see call volume and the share of clearly-negative calls behind each point.

frame.title: Emerging topics (sentiment change WoW)
frame.description: Topics ranked by week-over-week sentiment change. Large negative Δ WoW values flag topics where customer mood deteriorated this week.

frame.title: AI-summarised calls (bottom 20 by sentiment)
frame.description: 20 most negative calls in the period with AI-generated summaries. Read these to understand the qualitative drivers behind the sentiment scores above.

lines:
### Forecast & Plan
_How should we staff next month?_

frame.title: Call volume forecast · next 30 days
frame.description: 30-day forecast of daily call volume with lower/upper confidence bounds. Use the upper-bound line for conservative staffing; the centre line is the point estimate.

frame.title: Avg daily volume (forecast)
frame.description: Average forecast call volume per day across the next 30 days. Sparkline shows the daily forecast trajectory.

frame.title: Avg agents needed per day
frame.description: Average FTE needed per day across the forecast horizon at the chosen Target SLA / AHT / Shrinkage. Live-recalculates when you change any scenario parameter above.

frame.title: Agents needed per day · scenario
frame.description: Daily FTE required across the 30-day forecast horizon, with lower/upper bounds matching the volume confidence band. Recomputes whenever scenario parameters change — staff to the upper bound for safety.

lines:
**Scenario formula:** `agents_needed = volume × AHT(s) / (shift_hours × 3600) / (1 − 0.5 × target_SLA) / (1 − shrinkage)`  
_Adjust the parameters above to model different staffing scenarios — every chart recomputes live. The `(1 − 0.5 × target_SLA)` term approximates Erlang-C occupancy headroom: tighter SLA needs more agents. Set shift_hours = 24 for 24/7 concurrent coverage, 8 for a typical agent shift._

# Changelog

Log of changes to the Meridian Customer Operations dashboard.

---

### 2026-04-30 — Companion Genie space created

Natural-language Q&A surface over the same metric view, with 53 parameterised SQL examples (31 trusted-asset candidates) and 16 embedded benchmarks visible in the Genie Benchmarks tab.

### 2026-04-22 — Dashboard created

Six pages — Overview, Operations, Agents, Voice of Customer, Forecast & Plan, Changelog — over `mv_calls`, `silver_calls_wide`, `gold_kpi_summary`, `gold_forecast_volume`, and `gold_calls_enriched`.
